signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: GBP/USD trades at 1.3319, pressured by a dominant USD outperformance theme as the G10 spread reaches a critical 1.317% gap. The greenback finds fresh bids following Houthi tanker attacks and rising oil prices, triggering a 'risk-off' USD demand that contrasts with the moderate negative bias in UK economic data (CESI net -7). While GBP/USD holds slightly above its SMA20 at 1.3362, the technical posture remains fragile below the SMA200 at 1.3400. Speculative positioning is currently net long +28,278 (64.8%), suggesting a crowded trade vulnerable to further liquidation if geopolitical tensions escalate. The neutral OIS carry differential (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%) offers no structural support for the pair. Watch for a close below 1.3310 to signal a test of the SMA50 cluster. Risk: A de-escalation in Middle East tensions or a hawkish BoE hold on July 30 triggers a short-squeeze in GBP.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Positioning + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 163.78, remaining well above the 160.00 level that previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings and now testing the readiness of FinMin Katayama for 'decisive action.' Despite JPY headline inflation jumping to 1.7%, the BoJ is expected to hold rates at 1.0% next week, maintaining a massive +264bp OIS carry advantage for the USD. The options market shows a moderate put skew with 25d RR at -1.36 (Saxo), signaling that institutional players are paying a premium for downside protection despite the spot trend. Speculative shorts are heavily crowded at net -105,238 (LF long% 28.0%), creating an asymmetric risk profile for a disorderly unwind if intervention occurs. Volatility is rising with IV at 7.2% vs HV30 at 3.8%, reflecting a +3.5% VRP as markets price in tail risks. BoJ holds at 1.0%.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Economic Surprise + Vol
signals.text: EUR/USD trades at 1.1387, positioned below its SMA20/50/200 cluster (1.1420 to 1.1642) in a bearish technical alignment. The pair faces persistent headwinds from a 'Strong Negative' economic surprise index (CESI net -41), indicating Eurozone data is consistently missing forecasts compared to the 'Strong Positive' USD print (+29). Speculative positioning has shifted into a net short of -74,637 (long% 33.3%), aligning with the +144bp OIS carry advantage favoring the USD. FX options are pricing an elevated premium with IV at 7.1% vs HV30 of 4.5%, suggesting the market is hedging for a break lower rather than a mean-reversion move. The mild put skew in 25d RR (-0.40) confirms a slight directional bias toward further EUR weakness. Risk: A hawkish ECB surprise on Sept 10 or an unexpected cooling in US CPI narrows the real rate differential (+1.47% USD vs +0.16% EUR).

signals.title: AUD/USD — Carry vs Sentiment + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD trades at 0.6978, supported by a -73bp OIS carry premium (favoring AUD) and a restrictive real policy rate of +0.39%. Retail sentiment shows a 59% short bias, but the critical signal emerges from the options market where the VRP is at an extreme +7.1% (IV 13.1% vs HV30 6.0%), the highest in the G10 space. This suggests massive demand for AUD downside protection despite the RBA's hawkish bias and speculative net long positioning (+21,946). The pair is currently holding above its SMA20 (0.6950) but below the SMA50 (0.7036), indicating a lack of directional momentum. The strong negative economic surprise index for Australia (CESI -14) acts as a persistent drag. Risk: A break of the SMA20 at 0.6950 triggers a liquidation of the spec long and a spike in realized volatility.
